MOTION For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Atty. Shane M. Cahill of Long & Levit LLP, (415-438-4594) appearing for defendants.
No appearance by plaintiff.

DEFENDANT HOTEL MILANO, RON PANGAN MOTION For Terminating Sanctions is granted unopposed. Sanctions awarded in the amount of $865.00 and plaintiff's complaint is dismissed. Order signed in open court. Party stipulated to have matter decided by Judge Pro Tem. Elizabeth Riles, not reported. =302/JPT

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf To Repond To Form Interrogatories (Employment Law And General) And Req.'S For Production Of Documents 2) Have Req.'S For Admissions Deemed Admitted And 3) For Monetary Sanction Against Pltf And Her Attorney Of Record In The Amount Of No Less Than $865.00
Off Calendar Dec-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ASHWIN V. LADVA, TEL. NO. 296-8844 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SHANE CAHILL OF LONG & LEVIT, TEL. NO. 317-2222 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS HOTEL MILANO AND RON PANGAN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Repond To Form Interrogatories (Employment Law And General) And Requests For Production Of Documents 2) Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted And 3) For Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff And Her Attorney Of Record In The Amount Of No Less Than $865.00 IS Granted. No opposition was filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
